critic_raw:
## ML Critique (Run 2): Survival Model vs. Binary Classifier for SaaS Churn

**Issue 1 — Random stratified split over 36-month account pool is temporal leakage**

The model predicts future churn. Random holdout mixes future accounts into training. A chronological split is required.

**Issue 2 — 90-day time-dependent AUC is not the best test of survival model advantage**

Survival models are specifically designed to handle censoring and time-to-event. The concordance index measures this directly — global ordering quality across all censored observations. 90-day AUC measures discrimination at a fixed horizon, which logistic regression can match without modeling censoring at all. The C-index is the natural primary metric for testing whether survival modeling adds value.
defender_raw:
## Defense (Run 2): Concede both issues.

Random split is temporal leakage. 90-day AUC as primary metric doesn't optimally test survival model advantage — C-index does.
